---
title: "Resources"
---

::: {.day-header}
# Resources
::: {.day-subtitle}
Guides, references, and where to go deeper after the workshops
:::
:::

## Drexel Tools & Access

- **[AI at Drexel](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai)** --- the university's central AI hub (start here).
- **[AI Tools + data classification](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools)** --- which tools are approved for which data (authoritative).
- **ChatGPT Edu** --- university-licensed; primary tool for the workshops. Use **GPT-5.5** when it appears in your model picker; otherwise use the current default/latest GPT model in the Drexel workspace. [Request access](https://servicedesk.drexel.edu/TDClient/277/Portal/Requests/ServiceDet?ID=10760). See also [Tools & Access](tools.qmd).
- **Microsoft Copilot Chat with Enterprise Data Protection** --- sign in with your Drexel M365 account; Drexel lists it as usable with **High Risk Data** when Enterprise Data Protection is active.
- **Microsoft 365 Copilot** --- integrated with Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams for licensed faculty and staff; Drexel lists it as usable with **High Risk Data**.
- **Zoom AI Companion** --- included with Drexel Zoom; meeting summaries, recaps, and action items. Drexel lists it as **High Risk Data** when not recorded or transcribed and **Low Risk Data** when recorded or transcribed.
- **Adobe Acrobat AI Assistant, PDF Spaces, Creative Cloud, and Express** --- document and visual tools; Drexel lists them as usable with **Moderate Risk Data**. Featured in [Workshop 4](workshop4.qmd).

## Drexel Policy & Training

- **[Guidance](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/pedagogy-guidance)** and **[Policies](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/pedagogy-guidance/policies)** --- university policy and best practices.
- **[Guidance for Staff](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/pedagogy-guidance/staff)** --- staff-specific guidance.
- **[AI Trainings](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/trainings)** --- Drexel workshops and professional development.
- **[Standing Committee on AI (SCAI)](mailto:SCAI@drexel.edu)** --- questions about AI at Drexel.
- For research data: consult your **IRB** and any **data use agreements** before entering data into any AI tool.

## Quick Reference

::: {.info-box}

**PTCF prompt recipe** --- Persona, Task, Context, Format. Name who the AI should be, what to do, the background it needs, and the shape of the output.

**Data self-check** --- What is the data classification? Does Drexel list this tool and use case for that classification? Could I use a de-identified or synthetic version? If any answer gives you pause, stop and check.

**Verify before it counts** --- AI can state wrong facts and invent citations. Check anything that goes into a real document or decision.

:::

## Good First Uses by Role

| If you... | Try AI for... |
|-----------|---------------|
| Manage admin, schedules, grants | Drafting emails and reminders, summarizing long threads, cleaning up notes into agendas, reformatting documents |
| Coordinate research | Meeting recaps with action items, drafting project updates, summarizing protocols and reports, first-draft plans |
| Teach and run a lab / faculty | Drafting and revising materials, summarizing literature on public topics, generating practice questions, reformatting slides |

## Public Health & AI --- Further Learning

- **[ASPPH --- AI for Public Health Framework Report](https://aspph.org/initiatives/ai-for-public-health/ai-framework-report/)** --- the association's framework for responsible, ethical AI use in public health research, practice, and education (the five-pillar framework).
- **World Health Organization (2021). _Ethics and Governance of Artificial Intelligence for Health._** [Available online](https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/9789240029200) --- authoritative, freely available guidance; the executive summary (pp. 1--12) is a good starting point.
- **Mollick, E. (2024). _Co-Intelligence: Living and Working with AI._** --- accessible introduction to working with AI tools. His blog, **[One Useful Thing](https://www.oneusefulthing.org/)**, has regular, practical updates on AI developments.

## Research-Staff Sessions (Coming Summer or Fall)

For research staff who code and analyze data, specialized sessions will cover:

- AI for **data cleaning** and reshaping
- Writing and debugging code in **Stata, R, and Python**
- Explaining and documenting analyses
- Reproducibility and good practice when AI writes code

Watch for announcements, or email [daq26@drexel.edu](mailto:daq26@drexel.edu) to suggest topics.

## Suggest a Topic

These workshops will repeat and expand. If there's a tool, task, or workflow you'd like covered, email [daq26@drexel.edu](mailto:daq26@drexel.edu).
